topic: A person discovers the tree they planted as a child has been cut down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of damp earth, once a comforting memory, now just stung my nostrils. I stood at the edge of the clearing, my fists clenched so tight my knuckles were white monuments to my fury. Where the towering oak used to stand, there was only a raw, jagged scar on the landscape. It was gone. Vanished. The memory of my small hands, digging the roots in, felt like a cruel joke now. A bitter taste coated my tongue. I kicked at a clump of broken branches, the sound sharp and brittle in the silence. Every shadow seemed to mock the effort it took to breathe. I didn't move to mourn; I moved to resent the emptiness.

[story 2]

"Well, look at that," I muttered, the words scraping my throat raw. My gaze swept over the stumps, the ragged remnants of what was once a sentinel. A deep, churning resentment settled in my gut, heavy and cold. I walked around the perimeter slowly, deliberately, tracing the outline of the missing tree with my eyes, as if trying to etch the betrayal into my retina. My jaw was tight, a constant, rigid line. I didn't cry. Tears felt like a weakness I couldn't afford. Instead, I focused on the damage, cataloging the carelessness, the sheer audacity of it all. A slow, simmering heat rose in my chest, demanding an outlet, a destructive release.

[story 3]

I crouched down, my knees protesting the movement, and ran a hand over the exposed, pale wood. It felt wrong—a desecration. A sharp, acidic feeling coiled in my stomach. I looked up at the canopy where shade used to pool, now just harsh, unfiltered sunlight. A low growl escaped my chest, involuntary and ugly. I stood up abruptly, shaking my head, trying to dislodge the image of the tree. It had been a promise, a small, earnest act of creation, and now it was erased. I turned away quickly, walking with a stiff, purposeful stride toward the edge of the woods, determined to find something else to focus my irritation upon, anything to smother the sharp, burning sensation inside.
